prerequisites: An Apple Ads (Apple Search Ads) account with administrator access to Account Settings and User Management.
An API user created in the Apple Ads UI, with a public/private key pair generated so that a client_id and client_secret can be produced for OAuth.
Your Apple Ads organization id (org_id), visible in the Apple Ads account.
A start_date (YYYY-MM-DD) marking the earliest report data to sync, and a timezone value of either UTC or ORTZ.
A token_refresh_endpoint value (Apple's OAuth token endpoint); leave it at the default unless you have a reason to override it.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (client_id + client_secret)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates to the Apple Ads Campaign Management API using the OAuth 2.0 client_credentials grant. An account administrator creates an API user and key pair in the Apple Ads UI, which yields the client_id and client_secret. The connector exchanges these at the token_refresh_endpoint (Apple's OAuth token endpoint) with scope 'searchadsorg' to obtain a short-lived access token, then sends that token plus the org_id on each API request.

setupSteps.title: Create an API user in Apple Ads
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Apple Ads as an administrator and go to Account Settings, then User Management. Invite or add a user with the API role so the account can authenticate programmatically against the Apple Ads Campaign Management API.

setupSteps.title: Generate the OAuth credentials
setupSteps.body: Generate a public/private key pair for the API user and upload the public key in the Apple Ads UI. Use this to produce the OAuth client_id and client_secret. Provide both as the connector's client_id and client_secret secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Find your organization id
setupSteps.body: Locate your Apple Ads organization id (org_id) in the Apple Ads account. Enter it in the required org_id config field. The org_id is sent on each API call to scope requests to your organization.

setupSteps.title: Configure the token endpoint and sync window
setupSteps.body: Set token_refresh_endpoint to Apple's OAuth token endpoint (keep the default unless you must override it); the connector requests a token with grant_type 'client_credentials' and scope 'searchadsorg'. Set start_date (YYYY-MM-DD) and timezone (UTC or ORTZ). Optionally set end_date, lookback_window (1 to 30 days, default 30, covering Apple Ads' 30-day attribution window), num_workers, and backoff_factor.

sources.title: Apple Search Ads API reference (Apple Developer)
Implementing OAuth for the Apple Search Ads API (Apple Developer)
